alt_forms.note: ⚠️ WH*: Appears in WH40K Japan. 移動 is entirely generic Japanese; the フェイズ suffix creates the WH-pattern compound. Using 移動段階 is safer.

definitions.gloss_en: the phase of a turn in which units may move on the battlefield
definitions.gloss_jp: {ユニット|ゆにっと}が{戦|せん}{場|じょう}を{移|い}{動|どう}できる{手|て}{番|ばん}の{段|だん}{階|かい}
definitions.explanation_en: A dedicated phase for moving units before combat begins. Movement allowances and terrain restrictions apply.
definitions.implicit_notes: 移動 (movement) is a completely generic Japanese word used in JWC wargame documentation, Fire Emblem (移動力 = movement stat), and everyday language. The WH concern is the specific 移動フェイズ compound, not the word 移動 itself.
definitions.orthography_notes: 移動 (idou): 移 (transfer/shift) + 動 (move). 段階 replaces フェイズ to avoid the WH40K Japan phase-naming pattern. JWC documentation uses 移動作戦 (movement operations) as independent evidence for 移動 in wargame contexts.
definitions.game_setting_notes: Standard phase in miniature wargames. WH40K Japan uses 移動フェイズ; using 移動段階 clearly distinguishes this project's terminology.

usage_jp: {移|い}{動|どう}{段|だん}{階|かい}に{ユニット|ゆにっと}を{動|うご}かす (move units in the moving phase)
comparative_notes: 移動フェイズ is the WH40K Japan term; lower IP risk than 突撃フェイズ but still a WH compound pattern. 移動段階 is recommended.

examples.japanese: {移|い}{動|どう}{段|だん}{階|かい}に{前|ぜん}{進|しん}する。

cross_references.note: フェイズ — why 段階 is preferred
前進 — advance; primary action in this phase
行軍 — march; extended movement action
